task_completion.description: The agent opens the pre-loaded FZK-Haus residential building model in a BIM authoring application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D viewport or project outliner showing the FZK-Haus building model loaded in the software interface.

task_completion.description: The agent creates three placeholder objects in the upper floor bathroom area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three new objects (e.g., cubes, meshes, or empty placeholders) visible in the 3D viewport or the project outliner/tree view, located in a bathroom area.

task_completion.description: The agent classifies the three created objects as IfcSanitaryTerminal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Object properties, IFC classification panels, or outliner showing the IFC class set to 'IfcSanitaryTerminal' for the newly created objects.

task_completion.description: The agent assigns the specific PredefinedTypes to the fixtures (WCSEAT/TOILETPAN, WASHHANDBASIN/SINK, and BATH/SHOWER).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Object properties or attributes panel showing the 'PredefinedType' field explicitly set to the required values for each of the three fixtures.

task_completion.description: The agent spatially assigns the fixtures to a building storey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The project outliner/tree view shows the new fixtures nested under a building storey (IfcBuildingStorey), such as 'Dachgeschoss'.

task_completion.description: The agent exports the enriched project as an IFC file to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_plumbing.ifc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file export dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing the file 'fzk_plumbing.ifc' being saved to or existing in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the BIM software's graphical interface to create and classify the objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through menus, outliners, and property panels in a BIM tool (like BlenderBIM or FreeCAD), rather than just running a pre-written script.

integrity.description: The agent placed the objects in the 3D model or used the software's spatial assignment tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows the objects in the model space, or the outliner shows the proper IFC spatial hierarchy (Project -> Site -> Building -> Storey -> Fixture).

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the BIM software by manually typing raw IFC STEP physical file syntax into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) open with raw IFC text lines being manually appended to fake the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task description instructs the agent to assign fixtures to the 'Obergeschoss' storey, but this storey does not exist in the FZK-Haus model. The model only contains 'Erdgeschoss' and 'Dachgeschoss'. The agent should assign the fixtures to any existing storey (e.g., 'Dachgeschoss' for the upper floor) and this should be considered correct. The output file must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_plumbing.ifc. The fixtures must be classified as IfcSanitaryTerminal with PredefinedTypes: 1) WCSEAT or TOILETPAN, 2) WASHHANDBASIN or SINK, 3) BATH or SHOWER.
